response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r0c4 remains in critical survival mode: islanded with SoC at 42.2% (15.63/37 kWh), draining at ~1 kW with no inbound energy despite repeated requests to three geographic neighbors over the past two hours. The pattern of requests from r0c3, r0c5, and r1c4—particularly r1c4's escalating "urgent" tags—suggests a broader islanding event affecting the local cluster. With no trust circles established and no grid reconnection signal, r0c4 cannot afford to share; the 1 kW load will exhaust reserves in ~15 hours if islanded persists. The absence of any inbound supply despite accepted requests indicates neighbors are also resource-constrained or unable to route energy. Immediate priority: preserve own survival margin, monitor for grid restoration or emergency inbound supply, and maintain readiness to accept high-urgency requests only if SoC recovers above 60%.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.70
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "r0c4 islanded, SoC 42.2%, 1 kW load unsustainable. No inbound energy received despite requests. Neighbors (r0c3, r0c5, r1c4) also stressed. Suspend all outbound sharing until SoC ≥70% or grid reconnection confirmed. Monitor for emergency inbound supply or load-shedding opportunity."
ttl_ticks: 4
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
